pr.title: Finish controller match with cursed fake match
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Finished matching sysdolphin/baselib/controller.c by getting HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 from 99.99% to 100%, then flipped controller.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The fix used a deliberately awkward/cursed source rewrite: small static inline vector helpers, an apparently redundant r = sq(x) assignment before recomputing r, expansion of atan2f_check behavior with fabs(x) == 0.0f handling, and an early return instead of a nested !(r < adc_th) block. Symbols were also tightened so several controller helper functions and sdata2 constants became local labels/symbols rather than exported HSD_Controller_* globals.
what_changed: Added static inline helpers sq(f32), vec2DSqDist(f32, f32), and vec2Dlen(s8, s8) in controller.c near HSD_PadClamp.
Reworked HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 local declarations from a combined f32 r, a, ha declaration to separate declarations.

Changed radius calculation from a direct sqrtf(((f32)x * (f32)x) + ((f32)y * (f32)y)) expression to r = sq(x); followed by r = vec2Dlen(x, y). The first assignment is overwritten but is consistent with the PR title's 'cursed fake match' wording.
Replaced a call to atan2f_check(y, x) with explicit logic: if fabs(x) == 0.0f, choose +/- pi/2 based on y; otherwise call atan2f(y, x) and cast to f32.
Changed the adc threshold branch from wrapping all button-direction checks in if (!(r < p->adc_th)) to an early return when r < p->adc_th.
Kept the same directional angle windows for left, down, right, up, and wraparound-left button setting, but moved them out of the nested threshold block.
Changed configure.py so sysdolphin/baselib/controller.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Changed HSD_PadClampCheck1, HSD_PadClampCheck3, HSD_PadADConvertCheck1, and HSD_PadCrossDir scopes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from global to local.
Renamed several sdata2 constants at 0x804DE5B0 through 0x804DE618 from HSD_Controller_804DE5xx globals to local compiler-style @185, @186, @187, @188, @190, @355 through @362, and @384 labels.
Added a local ...bss.0 label at the HSD_PadLibData .bss address 0x804C1F78.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers to reshape floating-point expression codegen without changing the intended vector-length computation.
Expanded atan2f_check manually only where needed for HSD_PadADConvertCheck1, preserving the zero-x special case while avoiding the previous helper call shape.
Converted the threshold guard into an early return, a common control-flow tactic when trying to match branch layout and nesting in decompilation.
Accepted a source-level fake/redundant assignment, r = sq(x), as a matching tactic rather than forcing semantically clean C that would not match.
Updated configure.py only after the decomp.dev report showed the controller unit/function reached a new match.
Cleaned up symbol visibility to local for internal controller helpers and compiler-generated constants, reducing misleading global names in the symbol map.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn near-matches, exact source cleanliness may be less important than expressing operations in the same sequence the compiler used; a redundant overwritten assignment can be a legitimate last-byte matching tactic when documented by context.
Inlining tiny math helpers can influence temporary placement, call shape, and float expression ordering enough to solve a remaining mismatch.
Replacing a helper wrapper like atan2f_check with explicit branch logic can match original codegen while preserving behavior for x == 0.
Early returns can better match branch structure than an equivalent negated enclosing condition.
When a file becomes fully matching, remember to update the build configuration from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
After matching local/static functions and constants, update symbols.txt scopes and labels so the symbol map reflects local compiler artifacts instead of invented globals.
naming_conventions: Local compiler-generated sdata2 constants use @number labels, e.g. @185, @186, @355, rather than invented HSD_Controller_804DE5xx global names.
Internal controller helper functions in symbols.txt can remain named by their HSD_Pad* function names but should have scope:local when they are not true globals.
Helper names added in source used concise math-style names: sq, vec2DSqDist, and vec2Dlen.
The PR preserves HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 as the function name in source and symbols, while changing its symbol scope to local.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce static inline wrappers around simple arithmetic to steer generated instruction ordering.
Use a redundant overwritten temporary assignment, r = sq(x), as a fake-match/codegen nudge.
Split combined local declarations into separate declarations to influence compiler allocation/order.
Replace direct sqrtf expression with a nested inline helper path: vec2Dlen -> sqrtf(vec2DSqDist(...)).
Manually inline atan2f_check behavior with fabs(x) == 0.0f and +/-1.5707963267948966 constants.
Transform if (!(condition)) { body } into if (condition) return; body to match branch layout.
Adjust symbols from global names to local @ labels when constants are actually compiler-generated local sdata2 entries.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/sysdolphin/baselib/controller HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 improved from 99.99% to 100.00%.
The bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 41.39% (+0.02%, +636 bytes) and linked code increased to 26.91% (+0.15%, +5876 bytes).
searchable_terms: Finish controller match with cursed fake match
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use redundant overwritten assignments as matching tactics.
Find other controller.c or sysdolphin/baselib PRs that changed NonMatching to Matching.
Search for uses of atan2f_check replacement or manual atan2f zero-x handling.
Search symbols.txt history for HSD_Controller_804DE5xx local @ label conversions.
Find decomp lessons around early return versus negated enclosing condition branch matching.
